title: How 100 Enterprise CIOs Are Building and Buying Gen AI in 2025
body: Survey of 100 Enterprise CIOs on Generative AI strategies
Focus on LLM budgets, model adoption, and implementation trends
2025 enterprise AI landscape analysis

title: Agenda: Key Themes
body: LLM Budget Growth and Allocation Shifts
Multi-Model Adoption Trends
Market Leadership and Emerging Players
Production Deployment Patterns
Third-Party Application Testing

title: LLM Budget Growth Exceeds Expectations
body: LLM budgets have grown ahead of enterprises' already-high expectations
Leaders expecting an average of ~75% growth over the next year
Budget expansion signals deepening enterprise commitment to Gen AI

title: Innovation Budget Allocation Drops Sharply
body: Innovation budgets dropped from a quarter of LLM spending to just 7%
Shift indicates move from experimentation to production deployment
Enterprises prioritizing operational AI over exploratory projects

title: Multi-Model Strategy Becomes Standard
body: 37% of respondents now use 5 or more models
This represents an increase from 29% last year
Growing diversification of LLM portfolios across enterprises

title: OpenAI Maintains Market Leadership Position
body: OpenAI maintains market leadership in enterprise deployments
Sustained dominance despite increasing competition
Continues to be primary LLM provider for enterprise use cases

title: Google and Anthropic Gain Ground
body: Google made considerable strides in enterprise adoption
Anthropic made considerable strides in enterprise adoption
Traditional and emerging players expanding market share

title: OpenAI's o3 Model Sees Rapid Production Adoption
body: 23% of enterprises surveyed already use OpenAI's o3 model in production
Fast uptake indicates strong enterprise confidence in new model
o3 deployment rate reflects OpenAI's established enterprise relationships

title: DeepSeek Production Deployment Remains Limited
body: Just 3% of enterprises use DeepSeek model in production
Significant gap compared to OpenAI's o3 model at 23%
Emerging Chinese model provider faces adoption barriers in surveyed enterprises

title: o3 vs DeepSeek: Production Deployment Gap
body: OpenAI o3: 23% production adoption rate
DeepSeek: 3% production adoption rate
7.6x difference highlights market position disparities

title: Customer Support Emerges as Primary Use Case
body: Over 90% of survey respondents are testing third-party apps for customer support use cases
Customer support represents the most widely explored Gen AI application
Near-universal experimentation indicates high strategic priority

title: Third-Party Application Testing at Scale
body: Over 90% of respondents testing third-party apps
Customer support identified as primary testing focus
Enterprises favoring pre-built solutions over custom development

title: 2025 Enterprise Gen AI Outlook
body: ~75% budget growth signals aggressive expansion plans
Shift from innovation (7%) to production deployment priorities
Multi-model strategies and third-party apps dominate implementation approach
OpenAI leads while Google, Anthropic, and others compete for enterprise share
